limitations: Operator chat timing inside this slice is approximate unless anchored to repo receipts.
The 0.6.25 package report proves build inventory only; it records distributed:false and does not establish live prerelease authority.

entries.speaker: operator
entries.message: The operator explicitly corrected the drift: the one-second sync seam was supposed to solve the playback issue, not be removed, and neither audio nor video should be treated as the authority on time.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: A first repo-only 0.6.25 draft wrongly retired the periodic sync seam again by following the old drift packet. That draft was caught before live raw deploy and replaced instead of becoming authority.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Built 0.6.25 cleanly after keeping the one-second sync cadence and moving correction authority onto a player-owned timeline instead of AudioSource.time.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Regenerated the 0.6.25 package inventory with -SkipVarDistribute and confirmed it remains build-only with distributed:false.

entries.speaker: assistant
entries.message: Deployed the raw direct core-player asset and plugin as the live 0.6.25 alpha dev_deploy seam after restoring the periodic sync seam and moving its correction authority onto a player-owned master timeline anchored by clockResyncOccurred.

decisions: 0.6.25 keeps the one-second sync cadence instead of retiring it.

The correction target for periodic sync is no longer AudioSource.time; the runtime now treats a player-owned master timeline as the authority and uses clockResyncOccurred to refresh that timeline anchor.
The live authority seam for this slice is the raw direct core-player dev_deploy asset plus plugin, not the hosted modern_tv witness seam and not the .var package.
openQuestions: Does the player-owned timeline plus clockResyncOccurred seam reduce fast-forward bursts and long-lived AV drift on the same problematic VR movie tests without reopening the earlier movie playback failure?
notes: This slice explicitly records the correction to the earlier drifted assumption: the bug was the authority model inside periodic sync, not the existence of periodic sync itself.
The 0.6.25 package report exists only as build inventory and does not compete with the raw direct core-player dev_deploy authority.
